title: OpenIM Android SDK overview
description: Organizes OpenIM Android SDK v3.8.3 capabilities by lifecycle, authentication, listeners, users, groups, messages, push notifications, logging, files, and model types.
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android overview
content: The OpenIM Android documentation follows the current OpenIM SDK capability structure and is based on the public methods exported by Android SDK v3.8.3. Each method page focuses on the Android call style, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries. This section records how the Android platform layer maps to OpenIM core capabilities. Platform method names and parameter wrappers may differ from other SDKs; when the platform layer differs from the core export, use the Android SDK v3.8.3 prototype shown on each page. Integration Model | Model | Description | | | | | Initialization | Configure the API address, WebSocket address, logs, local data directory, and connection listener. | | Login state | Register message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login to avoid missing synchronization events. | | Asynchronous result | Platform methods return results through Promise, Future, or callback style depending on the method page. | | Message creation | Create Message methods create local message objects that are passed to sending methods. | | operationID | Generate a unique operationID for each call so client and server logs can be correlated. | Current Capability Pages | Page | Coverage | | | | | Android integration flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/getting started/send first message | Initialization, listeners, login, and the order of prerequisites before sending messages. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/overview | SDK initialization, login, logout, login state, foreground/background state, and network changes. | | Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/authenticating a user/authentication | Token handling, connection listener behavior, login status, and logout boundaries. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/overview | Connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV callbacks. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/overview | User profiles, user commands, online status subscriptions, friends, and blocklist APIs. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/group/overview | Group creation, joining, members, applications, moderation, and ownership transfer. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/overview | Conversation lists, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and typing state. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/push notifications/overview | FCM token updates and application badge management. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/logger/overview | SDK log writing and log upload. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/file/overview | Uploading files to the object storage path configured for the SDK. | | Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/models/overview | Data models, enums, listeners, and callback structures. | | Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/error codes | SDK core, server, and WebSocket protocol error code identifiers. | | Unsupported or unexposed capabilities /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/deprecated | Capabilities that are not currently exported as standalone Android SDK APIs. | Currently Unexposed Capabilities Android SDK v3.8.3 does not currently expose standalone methods for open groups, polls, scheduled messages, message threads, translation, independent reporting, group metadata, or counters. Add those pages after the SDK exports matching methods.

title: Android integration flow
description: Android integration flow for the OpenIM Android SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android getting-started send-first-message
content: This page describes the integration order and the parameters you need to prepare. For exact API signatures, open the corresponding capability pages. Call Order | Step | Required method | Description | | | | | | Read version | GetSdkVersion | Use this for diagnosing the current AAR or core binding version. | | Initialize | InitSDK listener, operationID, config | config passes the IMConfig JSON, and listener handles connection lifecycle events. | | Set listeners | SetConversationListener , SetAdvancedMsgListener , SetFriendListener | Set listeners before login to avoid missing events during login synchronization. | | Login | Login callback, operationID, userID, token | The token should be issued by your business server. Call conversation, message, and group APIs after login succeeds. | | Create message | CreateTextMessage , CreateImageMessage , and other creation methods | Creation methods synchronously return a message JSON string. | | Send message | SendMessage or SendMessageNotOss | Use recvID for one to one chats and groupID for group chats. Send results are returned through SendMsgCallBack . | | Logout or release | Logout , UnInitSDK | Use Logout for the current user and UnInitSDK to release global SDK resources. | Initialization Config | Field | Type | Description | | | | | | systemType | string | System type for logs and runtime identification. | | platformID | int32 | Platform ID. Android must pass a non zero value. | | apiAddr | string | OpenIM HTTP API address. Include the HTTP scheme. | | wsAddr | string | OpenIM WebSocket address. Include the WS scheme. | | dataDir | string | Local database, cache, and SDK data directory. | | logLevel | uint32 | SDK log level. | | isLogStandardOutput | bool | Whether logs are printed to standard output. | | logFilePath | string | Directory for log files. | | isExternalExtensions | bool | Whether external extension field handling is enabled. | Integration Checklist | Check | Passing condition | | | | | Initialize once | Repeated initialization reuses the existing UserForSDK ; do not rebuild it frequently in the same process. | | Non empty operationID | The core call chain validates operationID ; generate a traceable ID for each business operation. | | Call user APIs after login | Most APIs return an error callback when the SDK is not initialized or the user is not logged in. | | Valid JSON parameters | userIDList , groupIDList , searchParam , req , and similar string parameters should contain JSON for the target structure. | | Listeners are not lost | Set Listener replaces the previous listener. Maintain distribution in your wrapper layer when multiple consumers need events. | Related Types Send Msg Call Back /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/models/send msg call back SendMsgCallBack : core binding message sending callback, usually mapped to OnMsgSendCallback in the Android call layer. Related Pages Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/overview Overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/overview Send messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/overview send messages

title: Authentication flow
description: Authentication flow for the OpenIM Android SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android application authenticating-a-user authentication
content: Authentication is centered on the user token issued by your business server. The client initializes the SDK, registers listeners, logs in with userID and token , and keeps UI state aligned with connection events and login state. Authentication Order | Step | Description | | | | | Prepare a token | Issue an OpenIM user token from your trusted business backend. Do not hard code tokens in the client. | | Initialize the SDK | Call InitSDK with API, WebSocket, log, and local data configuration. | | Register listeners | Set connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login. | | Login | Call Login with the user ID and token. User scoped APIs are reliable only after login succeeds. | | Handle expiration | When token expiration or invalid token events arrive, request a new token from your backend and log in again. | Token Boundaries User tokens are issued by your backend and should be treated as sensitive credentials. Token expiration, invalid token, and kicked offline events are delivered through the connection listener. Calling Logout clears the current user session but does not release the global SDK instance. Related Pages Init SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/init sdk Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/login Logout /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/logout On Conn Listener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/models/on conn listener

title: Get SDK version
description: Explains the Android core binding method GetSdkVersion, including purpose, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries.
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android application methods get-sdk-version
content: Use GetSdkVersion for get sdk version in the OpenIM Android SDK. Feature Reads get sdk version from the local SDK state or OpenIM service according to the method contract. Initialize the SDK before calling user scoped APIs, and pass a unique operationID whenever the core binding signature includes it so client and server logs can be correlated. Core Binding Method | Item | Description | | | | | Method signature | GetSdkVersion string | | Capability | SDK lifecycle | | Source file | open im sdk/init login.go | Return Value The result is returned through the callback. Success is delivered through OnSuccess , failure through OnError , and sending or upload methods may also report progress.

title: Init SDK
description: Explains the Android core binding method InitSDK, including purpose, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries.
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android application methods init-sdk
content: Use InitSDK for init sdk in the OpenIM Android SDK. Feature InitSDK provides the Android SDK operation for init sdk. title: OpenIM Flutter SDK overview
description: Flutter SDK v3.8.3 documentation organized by OpenIM SDK capabilities.
keywords: sdk flutter v4 chat sdk v4 flutter overview
content: This documentation set follows the current OpenIM SDK capability model and the public methods exposed by Flutter SDK v3.8.3. Each method page covers the platform call shape, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries. The pages describe how the platform wrapper maps to OpenIM core capabilities. Platform method names and parameter wrappers can differ; when the wrapper differs from the core export, follow the platform SDK v3.8.3 signature shown on the method page. Integration Model | Model | Description | | | | | Initialization | Configure the API address, WebSocket address, local data directory, logging, and connection listener. | | Login state | Register listeners before login when possible, then call the login API with a backend issued token. | | Async result | Platform methods return results through Promise, Future, or callback patterns depending on the SDK. | | Message creation | Create local message objects with Create Message methods before sending them. | | operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation so client and server logs can be correlated. | Capability Pages | Page | Scope | | | | | Integration flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/getting started/send first message | Initialization, listeners, login, and the first message sequence. | | SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/overview | SDK initialization, login, logout, login status, and foreground/background state. | | Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/authenticating a user/authentication | Token handling, connection listener behavior, login status, and logout boundaries. | | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/overview | Connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV callbacks. | | User /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/overview | User profiles, user commands, and presence subscription. | | Friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/overview friends | Friend list, friend info, friend search, friend applications, and relationship updates. | | Blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/overview blacklist | Add, list, and remove blacklist entries. | | Group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/group/overview | Group creation, applications, membership, members, moderation, and ownership. | | Conversations and messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/overview | Conversation lists, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and typing state. | | Push notifications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/push notifications/overview | FCM token and application badge updates. | | Logger /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/logger/overview | SDK log configuration, writing client logs, and uploading logs. | | File upload /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/file/overview | Upload files through the object storage path configured for the SDK. | | Models /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/models/overview | Common models, enums, listeners, and callbacks. | | Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/error codes | SDK core, server, and WebSocket protocol marker error code enumeration. | | Unsupported or unexposed capabilities /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/deprecated | Capability boundaries that are not exposed by the current SDK. | Currently Unavailable Capabilities Flutter SDK v3.8.3 does not expose standalone methods for public group variants, polls, scheduled messages, message threads, translation, independent reports, group metadata, or metacounters. Add these pages only after the platform SDK exposes corresponding methods.

title: Flutter integration flow
description: Explains the Flutter integration flow method in Flutter SDK v3.8.3, including purpose, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries.
keywords: sdk flutter v4 chat sdk v4 flutter getting-started send-first-message
content: This page explains the Flutter sequence from SDK initialization to sending the first message. See each method page for exact signatures. Call Sequence | Step | Required API | Description | | | | | | Initialize | SDK initialization API | Configure apiAddr , wsAddr , data directory, logging, and the connection listener. | | Register listeners | Conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners | Register listeners before login when possible. | | Log in | Login API | Use a token issued by your business backend. Call business APIs only after login succeeds. | | Create a message | createTextMessage / CreateTextMessage and related methods | Create a local message object or JSON payload. | | Send the message | sendMessage / SendMessage | Pass a user ID for one to one chat or a group ID for group chat. | | Log out or uninitialize | Logout and uninitialize APIs | Log out the current user or release global SDK resources. | Initialization Config | Field | Type | Description | | | | | | platformID | number | Platform ID expected by the Flutter SDK wrapper. | | apiAddr | string | OpenIM HTTP API address. | | wsAddr | string | OpenIM WebSocket address. | | dataDir | string | Local database, cache, and SDK data directory. | | logLevel | number | SDK log level. | | isLogStandardOutput | boolean | Whether logs are written to standard output. | | logFilePath | string | Log file directory. | Checklist | Check | Expected result | | | | | Initialize once | Avoid recreating the SDK instance repeatedly in the same process. | | Trace operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation. | | Call after login | Most business APIs require initialization and login. | | Validate parameters | Request objects and message objects must match the platform type definitions. | | Keep listeners stable | Listener setters usually replace previous listeners. Add your own fan out layer if needed. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/overview Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/sending messages/overview

title: Overview
description: Explains the SDK lifecycle for Flutter SDK v3.8.3, including initialization, login, logout, login status, and runtime state.
keywords: sdk flutter v4 chat sdk v4 flutter application overview
content: SDK lifecycle covers initialization, login, logout, login status, foreground/background state, and network change notification. Register required listeners after initialization and before login whenever possible. Lifecycle Order | Stage | Method | Description | | | | | | Version record | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/get sdk version | Record the SDK version in startup logs, support tickets, and compatibility checks. | | Initialization | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/init sdk | Create the SDK instance, logging, connection management, local data directory, and base resources. | | Listeners | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/overview | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Login | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a token issued by your backend service. | | State query | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/get login status / Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/get login user id | Decide whether user scoped conversation, message, group, and friend APIs can be called. | | Runtime state | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/set app background status / Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK when app or network state changes. | | Logout | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/logout | End the current user session without destroying the SDK instance. | | Release | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/un init sdk | Release global SDK resources when the app leaves IM capability entirely or tests recreate the instance. | Boundaries | Boundary | Description | | | | | Initialization creates the runtime environment | Successful initialization does not mean the user is logged in. User, friend, group, conversation, and message APIs usually require login. | | Login depends on a backend token | The client should not mint tokens. Refresh expired or invalid tokens through your backend, then log in again. | | Logout is not SDK release | Logout ends the current user session; UnInitSDK releases the global SDK instance. | | operationID should be traceable | Generate operationID values that can correlate client logs, server logs, and support tickets. | Method Index | Method | Description | | | | | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/get sdk version | Get the current core SDK version. | | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/init sdk | Initialize the SDK instance and base resources. | | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/un init sdk | Release the current SDK instance. | | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a backend issued token. | | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/logout | Log out the current user. | | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/set app background status | Notify the SDK that the app entered foreground or background. | | Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK that network status changed. | | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/get login status | Query the current SDK login status. | | Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/get login user id | Get the user ID recorded by the current SDK instance. | Related Pages Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/authenticating a user/authentication Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/error codes

title: Authentication flow
description: Explains the authentication flow, token boundaries, and login-state handling for Flutter SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk flutter v4 chat sdk v4 flutter application authenticating-a-user authentication
content: Authentication uses a user token issued by your backend service. The client initializes the SDK, registers listeners, logs in with userID and token, and keeps UI state in sync with connection events and login status. Authentication Order | Step | Action | Description | | | | | | 1 | Get userID and token | Request the current user's OpenIM userID and token from your backend service. | | 2 | Initialize the SDK | Call Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/init sdk with API address, WebSocket address, platform information, and log settings. | | 3 | Register listeners | Register at least the connection listener. Register conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners before login when those events are needed. | | 4 | Log in | Call Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/login with userID and token. | | 5 | Handle state | Use connection callbacks, Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/get login status , and API results to decide when user scoped APIs are available. | | 6 | Logout or refresh token | When the token expires, becomes invalid, or the user is kicked offline, call Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/methods/logout and refresh the token before logging in again if needed. | Token Boundaries | Scenario | Handling | | | | | Token expired | Refresh the token through your backend, then call login again. | | Token invalid | Check userID, platform, issuing environment, and server time. | | Kicked offline | Notify the user through connection or login state events, then follow your account switch or re login policy. | | User logs out | Call Logout , then clear app level user state and page state. | Related Error Codes | Code | Meaning | | | | | 1501 | Token expired. | | 1502 | Token invalid. | | 1503 | Token malformed. | | 1506 | Token account was kicked offline. | | 1507 | Token does not exist. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/error codes

title: Overview
description: Explains event handler registration timing, event scope, and app-layer dispatch guidance for Flutter SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk flutter v4 chat sdk v4 flutter event-handler overview
content: Event handlers deliver connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV changes to the app layer. Register them before login when possible so initial sync events are not missed. Handler Index | Handler | Use case | | | | | Listen for group events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set group listener | Group info, members, applications, and permission changes. | | Listen for conversation events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set conversation listener | Conversation creation, updates, deletion, unread counts, and drafts. | | Listen for single message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set advanced msg listener | New messages, revocation, read status, and message extension changes. | | Listen for batch message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set batch msg listener | Batched new messages or batched sync results. | | Listen for user events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set user listener | Current user profile and user related state changes. | | Listen for friend events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set friend listener | Friend list, friend info, friend applications, and blacklist changes. | | Listen for custom business events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set custom business listener | Custom business events delivered by the server. | | Listen for message KV changes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/methods/set message kv info listener | Message extension key value additions, updates, and deletion. | Registration Guidance | Guidance | Description | | | | | Register before login | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Fan out in app code | If multiple pages consume the same event type, dispatch it through an app level event bus or state store. | | Avoid accidental replacement | Most Set Listener calls replace the listener stored for that event category. Repeated registration can overwrite the previous listener. | | Keep cache in sync | Listener callbacks should update local cache and UI state, then notify screens that need to refresh. |

title: Overview
description: User, friends, blacklist, and presence method index for Flutter SDK v3.8.3.

content: This page covers user profile, user command, presence subscription, friend, and blacklist APIs. Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. User Profile And Presence | Method | Description | | | | | Get self user info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/get self user info | Get the current user's profile for profile pages, startup refresh, or account switching. | | Update self info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/set self info | Update the current user's profile, such as nickname, avatar, or extension fields. | | Get users info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/get users info | Batch query public user profiles for conversation lists, message sender info, or friend refresh. | | Add user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/add user command | Add a lightweight user side command or marker. | | Delete user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/delete user command | Delete a user command by type and UUID. | | Get all user commands /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/get all user commands | Get all user commands under a specified type. | | Subscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/subscribe users status | Subscribe to online status for a group of users. | | Unsubscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/unsubscribe users status | Stop receiving online status for specified users. | | Get subscribed user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/get subscribe users status | Get the current status snapshot for subscribed users. | | Get user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/methods/get user status | Query online status for specified users. | Friends | Method | Description | | | | | Get specified friends info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/get specified friends info | Get profile details for specified friends. | | Get friend list /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/get friend list | Get the current user's full friend list. | | Get friend list by page /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/get friend list page | Page through the current user's friend list. | | Search friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/search friends | Search friends by keyword. | | Check friendship /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/check friend | Check friend relationship status. | | Add friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/add friend | Send a friend request. | | Update friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/update friends | Update friend profile fields controlled by the current user. | | Delete friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/delete friend | Delete a friend relationship. | | Get received friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as recipient | Get friend requests received by the current user. | | Get sent friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as applicant | Get friend requests sent by the current user. | | Accept friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/accept friend application | Accept a received friend request. | | Refuse friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/refuse friend application | Refuse a received friend request. | | Get unhandled friend application count /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/friend/methods/get friend application unhandled count | Get the number of unhandled friend requests. | Blacklist | Method | Description | | | | | Add to blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/blacklist/methods/add black | Add a user to the blacklist. | | Get blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/blacklist/methods/get black list | Get the current blacklist. | | Remove from blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/user/blacklist/methods/remove black | Remove a user from the blacklist. |

title: Overview
description: Conversation and message method index for Flutter SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk flutter v4 chat sdk v4 flutter message overview
content: This page covers conversations and messages: conversation lists, conversation settings, typing state, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and local messages. Main Flow | Stage | Methods | | | | | Create message | Use Create Message methods to create local message objects. | | Send message | Send with Send message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/sending messages/send message or Send message without OSS /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/sending messages/send message not oss . | | Receive message | Register message listeners from Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/event handler/overview . | | Query history | Use history and local search APIs under retrieving messages. | | Maintain read state | Mark conversation or message IDs as read. | Conversation Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Listing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/listing conversations/get all conversation list | Get all, paged, single, multiple, search, and generate conversation ID. | | Managing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/managing conversations/set conversation | Set conversation options, hide conversations, and update drafts. | | Conversation state /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/conversation state/get total unread msg count | Unread counts, input state, and typing status. | Message Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Creating messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/creating messages/create text message | Text, @ text, location, custom, quote, card, face, forward, merge, media, and file messages. | | Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/sending messages/send message | Send regular messages or send without OSS. | | Retrieving messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/retrieving messages/get advanced history message list | History message lists, reverse history, message list lookup, and local search. | | Managing messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/managing messages/revoke message | Revoke messages and set local message extension. | | Read status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/message/read status/mark conversation message as read | Mark conversation messages, all conversations, or selected title: OpenIM uni-app SDK overview
description: uni-app SDK v3.8.3 documentation organized by OpenIM SDK capabilities.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp overview
content: This documentation set follows the current OpenIM SDK capability model and the public methods exposed by uni app SDK v3.8.3. Each method page covers the platform call shape, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries. The pages describe how the platform wrapper maps to OpenIM core capabilities. Platform method names and parameter wrappers can differ; when the wrapper differs from the core export, follow the platform SDK v3.8.3 signature shown on the method page. Integration Model | Model | Description | | | | | Initialization | Configure the API address, WebSocket address, local data directory, logging, and connection listener. | | Login state | Register listeners before login when possible, then call the login API with a backend issued token. | | Async result | Platform methods return results through Promise, Future, or callback patterns depending on the SDK. | | Message creation | Create local message objects with Create Message methods before sending them. | | operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation so client and server logs can be correlated. | Capability Pages | Page | Scope | | | | | Integration flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/getting started/send first message | Initialization, listeners, login, and the /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/authenticating a user/authentication | Token handling, connection listener behavior, login status, and logout boundaries. | | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/overview | Connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV callbacks. | | User /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/overview | updates. | | Blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/overview blacklist | Add, list, and remove blacklist entries. | | Group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/overview | Group creation, applications, membership, members, moderation, and ownership. | | Conversations and messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/overview | Conversation lists, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and typing state. | | Push notifications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/push notifications/overview | FCM token and application badge updates. | | Logger /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/logger/overview | SDK log configuration, writing client logs, and uploading logs. | | File upload /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/file/overview | Upload files through the object storage path configured for the SDK. | | Models /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/models/overview | Common models, enums, listeners, and callbacks. | | Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/error codes | SDK core, server, and WebSocket protocol marker error code enumeration. | | Unsupported or unexposed capabilities /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/deprecated | Capability boundaries that are not exposed by the current SDK. | Currently Unavailable Capabilities uni app SDK v3.8.3 does not expose standalone methods for public group variants, polls, scheduled messages, message threads, translation, independent reports, group metadata, or metacounters. title: uni-app integration flow
description: Explains the uni-app integration flow method in uni-app SDK v3.8.3, including purpose, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp getting-started send-first-message
content: This page explains the uni app sequence from SDK initialization to sending the first message. See each method page for exact signatures. Call Sequence | Step | Required API | Description | | | | | | Initialize | SDK initialization API | Configure apiAddr , wsAddr , data directory, logging, and the connection listener. | | Register listeners | Conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners | Register listeners before login when possible. | | Log in | Login API | Use a token issued by your business backend. Call business APIs only after login succeeds. | | Create a message | createTextMessage / CreateTextMessage and related methods | Create a local message object or JSON payload. | | Send the message | sendMessage / SendMessage | Pass a user ID for one to one chat or a group ID for group chat. | | Log out or uninitialize | Logout and uninitialize APIs | Log out the current user or release global SDK resources. | Initialization Config | Field | Type | Description | | | | | | platformID | number | Platform ID expected by the uni app SDK wrapper. | | apiAddr | string | OpenIM HTTP API address. | | wsAddr | string | OpenIM WebSocket address. | | dataDir | string | Local database, cache, and SDK data directory. | | logLevel | number | SDK log level. | | isLogStandardOutput | boolean | Whether logs are written to standard output. | | logFilePath | string | Log file directory. | Checklist | Check | Expected result | | | | | Initialize once | Avoid recreating the SDK instance repeatedly in the same process. | | Trace operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation. | | Call after login | Most business APIs require initialization and login. | | Validate parameters | Request objects and message objects must match the platform type definitions. | | Keep listeners stable | Listener setters usually replace previous listeners. Add your own fan out layer if needed. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/overview Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/sending messages/overview

title: Overview
description: Explains the SDK lifecycle for uni-app SDK v3.8.3, including initialization, login, logout, login status, and runtime state.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp application overview
content: SDK lifecycle covers initialization, login, logout, login status, foreground/background state, and network change notification. Register required listeners after initialization and before login whenever possible. Lifecycle Order | Stage | Method | Description | | | | | | Version record | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/get sdk version | Record the SDK version in startup logs, support tickets, and compatibility checks. | | Initialization | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/init sdk | Create the SDK instance, logging, connection management, local data directory, and base resources. | | Listeners | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/overview | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Login | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a token issued by your backend service. | | State query | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/get login status / Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/get login user id | Decide whether user scoped conversation, message, group, and friend APIs can be called. | | Runtime state | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/set app background status / Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK when app or network state changes. | | Logout | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/logout | End the current user session without destroying the SDK instance. | | Release | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/un init sdk | Release global SDK resources when the app leaves IM capability entirely or tests recreate the instance. | Boundaries | Boundary | Description | | | | | Initialization creates the runtime environment | Successful initialization does not mean the user is logged in. User, friend, group, conversation, and message APIs usually require login. | | Login depends on a backend token | The client should not mint tokens. Refresh expired or invalid tokens through your backend, then log in again. | | Logout is not SDK release | Logout ends the current user session; UnInitSDK releases the global SDK instance. | | operationID should be traceable | Generate operationID values that can correlate client logs, server logs, and support tickets. | Method Index | Method | Description | | | | | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/get sdk version | Get the current core SDK version. | | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/init sdk | Initialize the SDK instance and base resources. | | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/un init sdk | Release the current SDK instance. | | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a backend issued token. | | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/logout | Log out the current user. | | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/set app background status | Notify the SDK that the app entered foreground or background. | | Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK that network status changed. | | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/get login status | Query the current SDK login status. | | Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/get login user id | Get the user ID recorded by the current SDK instance. | Related Pages Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/authenticating a user/authentication Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/error codes

title: Authentication flow
description: Explains the authentication flow, token boundaries, and login-state handling for uni-app SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp application authenticating-a-user authentication
content: Authentication uses a user token issued by your backend service. The client initializes the SDK, registers listeners, logs in with userID and token, and keeps UI state in sync with connection events and login status. Authentication Order | Step | Action | Description | | | | | | 1 | Get userID and token | Request the current user's OpenIM userID and token from your backend service. | | 2 | Initialize the SDK | Call Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/init sdk with API address, WebSocket address, platform information, and log settings. | | 3 | Register listeners | Register at least the connection listener. Register conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners before login when those events are needed. | | 4 | Log in | Call Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/login with userID and token. | | 5 | Handle state | Use connection callbacks, Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/get login status , and API results to decide when user scoped APIs are available. | | 6 | Logout or refresh token | When the token expires, becomes invalid, or the user is kicked offline, call Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/methods/logout and refresh the token before logging in again if needed. | Token Boundaries | Scenario | Handling | | | | | Token expired | Refresh the token through your backend, then call login again. | | Token invalid | Check userID, platform, issuing environment, and server time. | | Kicked offline | Notify the user through connection or login state events, then follow your account switch or re login policy. | | User logs out | Call Logout , then clear app level user state and page state. | Related Error Codes | Code | Meaning | | | | | 1501 | Token expired. | | 1502 | Token invalid. | | 1503 | Token malformed. | | 1506 | Token account was kicked offline. | | 1507 | Token does not exist. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/error codes

title: Overview
description: Explains event handler registration timing, event scope, and app-layer dispatch guidance for uni-app SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp event-handler overview
content: Event handlers deliver connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV changes to the app layer. Register them before login when possible so initial sync events are not missed. Handler Index | Handler | Use case | | | | | Listen for group events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set group listener | Group info, members, applications, and permission changes. | | Listen for conversation events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set conversation listener | Conversation creation, updates, deletion, unread counts, and drafts. | | Listen for single message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set advanced msg listener | New messages, revocation, read status, and message extension changes. | | Listen for batch message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set batch msg listener | Batched new messages or batched sync results. | | Listen for user events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set user listener | Current user profile and user related state changes. | | Listen for friend events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set friend listener | Friend list, friend info, friend applications, and blacklist changes. | | Listen for custom business events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set custom business listener | Custom business events delivered by the server. | | Listen for message KV changes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/methods/set message kv info listener | Message extension key value additions, updates, and deletion. | Registration Guidance | Guidance | Description | | | | | Register before login | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Fan out in app code | If multiple pages consume the same event type, dispatch it through an app level event bus or state store. | | Avoid accidental replacement | Most Set Listener calls replace the listener stored for that event category. Repeated registration can overwrite the previous listener. | | Keep cache in sync | Listener callbacks should update local cache and UI state, then notify screens that need to refresh. |

title: Overview

content: This page covers user profile, user command, presence subscription, friend, and blacklist APIs. Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. User Profile And Presence | Method | Description | | | | | Get self user info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/get self user info | Get the current user's profile for profile pages, startup refresh, or account switching. | | Update self info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/set self info | Update the current user's profile, such as nickname, avatar, or extension fields. | | Get users info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/get users info | Batch query public user profiles for conversation lists, message sender info, or friend refresh. | | Add user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/add user command | Add a lightweight user side command or marker. | | Delete user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/delete user command | Delete a user command by type and UUID. | | Get all user commands /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/get all user commands | Get all user commands under a specified type. | | Subscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/subscribe users status | Subscribe to online status for a group of users. | | Unsubscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/unsubscribe users status | Stop receiving online status for specified users. | | Get subscribed user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/get subscribe users status | Get the current status snapshot for subscribed users. | | Get user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/methods/get user status | Query online status for specified users. | Friends | Method | Description | | | | | Get specified friends info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/get specified friends info | Get profile details for specified friends. | | Get friend list /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/get friend list | Get the current user's full friend list. | | Get friend list by page /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/get friend list page | Page through the current user's friend list. | | Search friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/search friends | Search friends by keyword. | | Check friendship /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/check friend | Check friend relationship status. | | Add friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/add friend | Send a friend request. | | Update friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/update friends | Update friend profile fields controlled by the current user. | | Delete friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/delete friend | Delete a friend relationship. | | Get received friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as recipient | Get friend requests received by the current user. | | Get sent friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as applicant | Get friend requests sent by the current user. | | Accept friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/accept friend application | Accept a received friend request. | | Refuse friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/refuse friend application | Refuse a received friend request. | | Get unhandled friend application count /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/friend/methods/get friend application unhandled count | Get the number of unhandled friend requests. | Blacklist | Method | Description | | | | | Add to blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/blacklist/methods/add black | Add a user to the blacklist. | | Get blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/blacklist/methods/get black list | Get the current blacklist. | | Remove from blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/user/blacklist/methods/remove black | Remove a user from the blacklist. |

Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. Managing Groups | Method | Description | | | | | Create group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/managing groups/create group | Create a group and optionally set initial members and administrators. | | Update group info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/managing groups/set group info | Update group profile fields such as name, announcement, introduction, and verification mode. | | Get group info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/managing groups/get specified groups info | Batch get group info for conversation lists or selected group refreshes. | | Search groups /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/managing groups/search groups | Search groups by keyword or group attributes. | | Transfer group owner /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/managing groups/transfer group owner | Transfer group ownership to another member. | | Dismiss group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/managing groups/dismiss group | Dismiss a group when the current user has permission. | Group Applications | Method | Description | | | | | Join group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group applications/join group | Apply to join a group. | | Get received group applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group applications/get group application list as recipient | Get group applications received by groups managed by the current user. | | Get sent group applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group applications/get group application list as applicant | Get group applications sent by the current user. | | Accept group application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group applications/accept group application | Approve a received group application. | | Refuse group application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group applications/refuse group application | Refuse a received group application. | | Get unhandled group application count /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group applications/get group application unhandled count | Get the number of unhandled group applications. | Group Membership | Method | Description | | | | | Get joined groups /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group membership/get joined group list | Get groups joined by the current user. | | Get joined groups by page /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group membership/get joined group list page | Page through groups joined by the current user. | | Check whether joined /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group membership/is join group | Check whether the current user has joined a group. | | Check users in group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group membership/get users in group | Check whether specified users are in a group. | | Quit group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group membership/quit group | Leave a group. | Group Members | Method | Description | | | | | Invite users to group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/invite user to group | Invite users to join a group. | | Kick group members /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/kick group member | Remove members from a group. | | Update group member info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/set group member info | Update member nickname, role, or other member profile fields. | | Get group member list /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/get group member list | Get group members by role or pagination. | | Filter members by join time /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/get group member list by join time filter | Filter group members by join time. | | Get owner and administrators /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/get group member owner and admin | Get the group owner and administrators. | | Get specified group member info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/get specified group members info | Get member profiles for specified group members. | | Search group members /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group members/search group members | Search members in a group. | Group Moderation | Method | Description | | | | | Set group mute /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group moderation/change group mute | Mute or unmute the whole group. | | Set group member mute /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/group/group moderation/change group member mute | title: Overview
description: Conversation and message method index for uni-app SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp message overview
content: This page covers conversations and messages: conversation lists, conversation settings, typing state, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and local messages. Main Flow | Stage | Methods | | | | | Create message | Use Create Message methods to create local message objects. | | Send message | Send with Send message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/sending messages/send message or Send message without OSS /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/sending messages/send message not oss . | | Receive message | Register message listeners from Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/event handler/overview . | | Query history | Use history and local search APIs under retrieving messages. | | Maintain read state | Mark conversation or message IDs as read. | Conversation Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Listing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/listing conversations/get all conversation list | Get all, paged, single, multiple, search, and generate conversation ID. | | Managing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/managing conversations/set conversation | Set conversation options, hide conversations, and update drafts. | | Conversation state /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/conversation state/get total unread msg count | Unread counts, input state, and typing status. | Message Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Creating messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/creating messages/create text message | Text, @ text, location, custom, quote, card, face, forward, merge, media, and file messages. | | Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/sending messages/send message | Send regular messages or send without OSS. | | Retrieving messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/retrieving messages/get advanced history message list | History message lists, reverse history, message list lookup, and local search. | | Managing messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/managing messages/revoke message | Revoke messages and set local message extension. | | Read status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/read status/mark conversation message as read | Mark conversation messages, all conversations, or selected message IDs as read. | | Deleting messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/deleting messages/delete message | Delete local messages, delete server messages, clear messages, and delete conversation messages. | | Local messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/uniapp/message/local messages/insert single message to local storage | Insert local single chat or group messages. |

title: OpenIM WASM SDK overview
description: WASM SDK v3.8.3 documentation organized by OpenIM SDK capabilities.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm overview
content: This documentation set follows the current OpenIM SDK capability model and the public methods exposed by WASM SDK v3.8.3. Each method page covers the platform call shape, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries. The pages describe how the platform wrapper maps to OpenIM core capabilities. Platform method names and parameter wrappers can differ; when the wrapper differs from the core export, follow the platform SDK v3.8.3 signature shown on the method page. Integration Model | Model | Description | | | | | Initialization | Configure the API address, WebSocket address, local data directory, logging, and connection listener. | | Login state | Register listeners before login when possible, then call the login API with a backend issued token. | | Async result | Platform methods return results through Promise, Future, or callback patterns depending on the SDK. | | Message creation | Create local message objects with Create Message methods before sending them. | | operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation so client and server logs can be correlated. | Capability Pages | Page | Scope | | | | | Integration flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message | Initialization, listeners, login, and the first message sequence. | | SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/overview | SDK initialization, login, logout, login status, and foreground/background state. | | Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication | Token handling, connection listener behavior, login status, and logout boundaries. | | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/overview | Connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV callbacks. | | User /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/overview | User profiles, user commands, and presence subscription. | | Friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/overview friends | Friend list, friend info, friend search, friend applications, and relationship updates. | | Blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/overview blacklist | Add, list, and remove blacklist entries. | | Group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/overview | Group creation, applications, membership, members, moderation, and ownership. | | Conversations and messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/overview | Conversation lists, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and typing state. | | Push notifications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/push notifications/overview | FCM token and application badge updates. | | Logger /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/logger/overview | SDK log configuration, writing client logs, and uploading logs. | | File upload /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/file/overview | Upload files through the object storage path configured for the SDK. | | Models /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/models/overview | Common models, enums, listeners, and callbacks. | | Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/error codes | SDK core, server, and WebSocket protocol marker error code enumeration. | | Unsupported or unexposed capabilities /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/deprecated | Capability boundaries that are not exposed by the current SDK. | Currently Unavailable Capabilities WASM SDK v3.8.3 does not expose standalone methods for public group variants, polls, scheduled messages, message threads, translation, independent reports, group metadata, or metacounters. Add these pages only after the platform SDK exposes corresponding methods.

title: WASM integration flow
description: Explains the WASM integration flow method in WASM SDK v3.8.3, including purpose, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm getting-started send-first-message
content: This page explains the WASM sequence from SDK initialization to sending the first message. See each method page for exact signatures. Call Sequence | Step | Required API | Description | | | | | | Initialize | SDK initialization API | Configure apiAddr , wsAddr , data directory, logging, and the connection listener. | | Register listeners | Conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners | Register listeners before login when possible. | | Log in | Login API | Use a token issued by your business backend. Call business APIs only after login succeeds. | | Create a message | createTextMessage / CreateTextMessage and related methods | Create a local message object or JSON payload. | | Send the message | sendMessage / SendMessage | Pass a user ID for one to one chat or a group ID for group chat. | | Log out or uninitialize | Logout and uninitialize APIs | Log out the current user or release global SDK resources. | Initialization Config | Field | Type | Description | | | | | | platformID | number | Platform ID expected by the WASM SDK wrapper. | | apiAddr | string | OpenIM HTTP API address. | | wsAddr | string | OpenIM WebSocket address. | | dataDir | string | Local database, cache, and SDK data directory. | | logLevel | number | SDK log level. | | isLogStandardOutput | boolean | Whether logs are written to standard output. | | logFilePath | string | Log file directory. | Checklist | Check | Expected result | | | | | Initialize once | Avoid recreating the SDK instance repeatedly in the same process. | | Trace operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation. | | Call after login | Most business APIs require initialization and login. | | Validate parameters | Request objects and message objects must match the platform type definitions. | | Keep listeners stable | Listener setters usually replace previous listeners. Add your own fan out layer if needed. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/overview Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending messages/overview

title: Overview
description: Explains the SDK lifecycle for WASM SDK v3.8.3, including initialization, login, logout, login status, and runtime state.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm application overview
content: SDK lifecycle covers initialization, login, logout, login status, foreground/background state, and network change notification. Register required listeners after initialization and before login whenever possible. Lifecycle Order | Stage | Method | Description | | | | | | Version record | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/get sdk version | Record the SDK version in startup logs, support tickets, and compatibility checks. | | Initialization | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/init sdk | Create the SDK instance, logging, connection management, local data directory, and base resources. | | Listeners | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/overview | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Login | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a token issued by your backend service. | | State query | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/get login status / Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/get login user id | Decide whether user scoped conversation, message, group, and friend APIs can be called. | | Runtime state | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/set app background status / Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK when app or network state changes. | | Logout | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/logout | End the current user session without destroying the SDK instance. | | Release | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/un init sdk | Release global SDK resources when the app leaves IM capability entirely or tests recreate the instance. | Boundaries | Boundary | Description | | | | | Initialization creates the runtime environment | Successful initialization does not mean the user is logged in. User, friend, group, conversation, and message APIs usually require login. | | Login depends on a backend token | The client should not mint tokens. Refresh expired or invalid tokens through your backend, then log in again. | | Logout is not SDK release | Logout ends the current user session; UnInitSDK releases the global SDK instance. | | operationID should be traceable | Generate operationID values that can correlate client logs, server logs, and support tickets. | Method Index | Method | Description | | | | | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/get sdk version | Get the current core SDK version. | | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/init sdk | Initialize the SDK instance and base resources. | | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/un init sdk | Release the current SDK instance. | | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a backend issued token. | | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/logout | Log out the current user. | | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/set app background status | Notify the SDK that the app entered foreground or background. | | Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK that network status changed. | | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/get login status | Query the current SDK login status. | | Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/get login user id | Get the user ID recorded by the current SDK instance. | Related Pages Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/error codes

title: Authentication flow
description: Explains the authentication flow, token boundaries, and login-state handling for WASM SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm application authenticating-a-user authentication
content: Authentication uses a user token issued by your backend service. The client initializes the SDK, registers listeners, logs in with userID and token, and keeps UI state in sync with connection events and login status. Authentication Order | Step | Action | Description | | | | | | 1 | Get userID and token | Request the current user's OpenIM userID and token from your backend service. | | 2 | Initialize the SDK | Call Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/init sdk with API address, WebSocket address, platform information, and log settings. | | 3 | Register listeners | Register at least the connection listener. Register conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners before login when those events are needed. | | 4 | Log in | Call Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/login with userID and token. | | 5 | Handle state | Use connection callbacks, Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/get login status , and API results to decide when user scoped APIs are available. | | 6 | Logout or refresh token | When the token expires, becomes invalid, or the user is kicked offline, call Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/methods/logout and refresh the token before logging in again if needed. | Token Boundaries | Scenario | Handling | | | | | Token expired | Refresh the token through your backend, then call login again. | | Token invalid | Check userID, platform, issuing environment, and server time. | | Kicked offline | Notify the user through connection or login state events, then follow your account switch or re login policy. | | User logs out | Call Logout , then clear app level user state and page state. | Related Error Codes | Code | Meaning | | | | | 1501 | Token expired. | | 1502 | Token invalid. | | 1503 | Token malformed. | | 1506 | Token account was kicked offline. | | 1507 | Token does not exist. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/error codes

title: Overview
description: Explains event handler registration timing, event scope, and app-layer dispatch guidance for WASM SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm event-handler overview
content: Event handlers deliver connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV changes to the app layer. Register them before login when possible so initial sync events are not missed. Handler Index | Handler | Use case | | | | | Listen for group events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set group listener | Group info, members, applications, and permission changes. | | Listen for conversation events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set conversation listener | Conversation creation, updates, deletion, unread counts, and drafts. | | Listen for single message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set advanced msg listener | New messages, revocation, read status, and message extension changes. | | Listen for batch message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set batch msg listener | Batched new messages or batched sync results. | | Listen for user events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set user listener | Current user profile and user related state changes. | | Listen for friend events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set friend listener | Friend list, friend info, friend applications, and blacklist changes. | | Listen for custom business events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set custom business listener | Custom business events delivered by the server. | | Listen for message KV changes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/methods/set message kv info listener | Message extension key value additions, updates, and deletion. | Registration Guidance | Guidance | Description | | | | | Register before login | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Fan out in app code | If multiple pages consume the same event type, dispatch it through an app level event bus or state store. | | Avoid accidental replacement | Most Set Listener calls replace the listener stored for that event category. Repeated registration can overwrite the previous listener. | | Keep cache in sync | Listener callbacks should update local cache and UI state, then notify screens that need to refresh. |

title: Overview
description: User, friends, blacklist, and presence method index for WASM SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user overview
content: This page covers user profile, user command, presence subscription, friend, and blacklist APIs. Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. User Profile And Presence | Method | Description | | | | | Get self user info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/get self user info | Get the current user's profile for profile pages, startup refresh, or account switching. | | Update self info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/set self info | Update the current user's profile, such as nickname, avatar, or extension fields. | | Get users info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/get users info | Batch query public user profiles for conversation lists, message sender info, or friend refresh. | | Add user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/add user command | Add a lightweight user side command or marker. | | Delete user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/delete user command | Delete a user command by type and UUID. | | Get all user commands /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/get all user commands | Get all user commands under a specified type. | | Subscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/subscribe users status | Subscribe to online status for a group of users. | | Unsubscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/unsubscribe users status | Stop receiving online status for specified users. | | Get subscribed user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/get subscribe users status | Get the current status snapshot for subscribed users. | | Get user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/methods/get user status | Query online status for specified users. | Friends | Method | Description | | | | | Get specified friends info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/get specified friends info | Get profile details for specified friends. | | Get friend list /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/get friend list | Get the current user's full friend list. | | Get friend list by page /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/get friend list page | Page through the current user's friend list. | | Search friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/search friends | Search friends by keyword. | | Check friendship /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/check friend | Check friend relationship status. | | Add friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/add friend | Send a friend request. | | Update friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/update friends | Update friend profile fields controlled by the current user. | | Delete friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/delete friend | Delete a friend relationship. | | Get received friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as recipient | Get friend requests received by the current user. | | Get sent friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as applicant | Get friend requests sent by the current user. | | Accept friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/accept friend application | Accept a received friend request. | | Refuse friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/refuse friend application | Refuse a received friend request. | | Get unhandled friend application count /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/friend/methods/get friend application unhandled count | Get the number of unhandled friend requests. | Blacklist | Method | Description | | | | | Add to blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/blacklist/methods/add black | Add a user to the blacklist. | | Get blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/blacklist/methods/get black list | Get the current blacklist. | | Remove from blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/blacklist/methods/remove black | Remove a user from the blacklist. |

content: This page covers OpenIM group creation, group profile management, group applications, membership, members, moderation, and ownership transfer. Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. Managing Groups | Method | Description | | | | | Create group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/managing groups/create group | Create a group and optionally set initial members and administrators. | | Update group info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/managing groups/set group info | Update group profile fields such as name, announcement, introduction, and verification mode. | | Get group info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/managing groups/get specified groups info | Batch get group info for conversation lists or selected group refreshes. | | Search groups /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/managing groups/search groups | Search groups by keyword or group attributes. | | Transfer group owner /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/managing groups/transfer group owner | Transfer group ownership to another member. | | Dismiss group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/managing groups/dismiss group | Dismiss a group when the current user has permission. | Group Applications | Method | Description | | | | | Join group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group applications/join group | Apply to join a group. | | Get received group applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group applications/get group application list as recipient | Get group applications received by groups managed by the current user. | | Get sent group applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group applications/get group application list as applicant | Get group applications sent by the current user. | | Accept group application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group applications/accept group application | Approve a received group application. | | Refuse group application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group applications/refuse group application | Refuse a received group application. | | Get unhandled group application count /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group applications/get group application unhandled count | Get the number of unhandled group applications. | Group Membership | Method | Description | | | | | Get joined groups /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group membership/get joined group list | Get groups joined by the current user. | | Get joined groups by page /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group membership/get joined group list page | Page through groups joined by the current user. | | Check whether joined /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group membership/is join group | Check whether the current user has joined a group. | | Check users in group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group membership/get users in group | Check whether specified users are in a group. | | Quit group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group membership/quit group | Leave a group. | Group Members | Method | Description | | | | | Invite users to group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/invite user to group | Invite users to join a group. | | Kick group members /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/kick group member | Remove members from a group. | | Update group member info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/set group member info | Update member nickname, role, or other member profile fields. | | Get group member list /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/get group member list | Get group members by role or pagination. | | Filter members by join time /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/get group member list by join time filter | Filter group members by join time. | | Get owner and administrators /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/get group member owner and admin | Get the group owner and administrators. | | Get specified group member info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/get specified group members info | Get member profiles for specified group members. | | Search group members /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group members/search group members | Search members in a group. | Group Moderation | Method | Description | | | | | Set group mute /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group moderation/change group mute | Mute or unmute the whole group. | | Set group member mute /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/group/group moderation/change group member mute |
