title: Overview
description: Conversation and message method index for WASM SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message overview
content: This page covers conversations and messages: conversation lists, conversation settings, typing state, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and local messages. Main Flow | Stage | Methods | | | | | Create message | Use Create Message methods to create local message objects. | | Send message | Send with Send message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending messages/send message or Send message without OSS /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending messages/send message not oss . | | Receive message | Register message listeners from Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/event handler/overview . | | Query history | Use history and local search APIs under retrieving messages. | | Maintain read state | Mark conversation or message IDs as read. | Conversation Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Listing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/listing conversations/get all conversation list | Get all, paged, single, multiple, search, and generate conversation ID. | | Managing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/managing conversations/set conversation | Set conversation options, hide conversations, and update drafts. | | Conversation state /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/conversation state/get total unread msg count | Unread counts, input state, and typing status. | Message Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Creating messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/creating messages/create text message | Text, @ text, location, custom, quote, card, face, forward, merge, media, and file messages. | | Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending messages/send message | Send regular messages or send without OSS. | | Retrieving messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/retrieving messages/get advanced history message list | History message lists, reverse history, message list lookup, and local search. | | Managing messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/managing messages/revoke message | Revoke messages and set local message extension. | | Read status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/read status/mark conversation message as read | Mark conversation messages, all conversations, or selected message IDs as read. | | Deleting messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/deleting messages/delete message | Delete local messages, delete server messages, clear messages, and delete conversation messages. | | Local messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/local messages/insert single message to local storage | Insert local single chat or group messages. |

title: OpenIM Electron SDK overview
description: Electron SDK v3.8.3 documentation organized by OpenIM SDK capabilities.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron overview
content: This documentation set follows the current OpenIM SDK capability model and the public methods exposed by Electron SDK v3.8.3. Each method page covers the platform call shape, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries. The pages describe how the platform wrapper maps to OpenIM core capabilities. Platform method names and parameter wrappers can differ; when the wrapper differs from the core export, follow the platform SDK v3.8.3 signature shown on the method page. Integration Model | Model | Description | | | | | Initialization | Configure the API address, WebSocket address, local data directory, logging, and connection listener. | | Login state | Register listeners before login when possible, then call the login API with a backend issued token. | | Async result | Platform methods return results through Promise, Future, or callback patterns depending on the SDK. | | Message creation | Create local message objects with Create Message methods before sending them. | | operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation so client and server logs can be correlated. | Capability Pages | Page | Scope | | | | | Integration flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/getting started/send first message | Initialization, listeners, login, and the first message sequence. | | SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/overview | SDK initialization, login, logout, login status, and foreground/background state. | | Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/authenticating a user/authentication | Token handling, connection listener behavior, login status, and logout boundaries. | | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/overview | Connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV callbacks. | | User /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/overview | User profiles, user commands, and presence subscription. | | Friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/overview friends | Friend list, friend info, friend search, friend applications, and relationship updates. | | Blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/overview blacklist | Add, list, and remove blacklist entries. | | Group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/group/overview | Group creation, applications, membership, members, moderation, and ownership. | | Conversations and messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/overview | Conversation lists, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and typing state. | | Push notifications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/push notifications/overview | FCM token and application badge updates. | | Logger /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/logger/overview | SDK log configuration, writing client logs, and uploading logs. | | File upload /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/file/overview | Upload files through the object storage path configured for the SDK. | | Models /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/models/overview | Common models, enums, listeners, and callbacks. | | Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/error codes | SDK core, server, and WebSocket protocol marker error code enumeration. | | Unsupported or unexposed capabilities /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/deprecated | Capability boundaries that are not exposed by the current SDK. | Currently Unavailable Capabilities Electron SDK v3.8.3 does not expose standalone methods for public group variants, polls, scheduled messages, message threads, translation, independent reports, group metadata, or metacounters. Add these pages only after the platform SDK exposes corresponding methods.

title: Electron integration flow
description: Explains the Electron integration flow method in Electron SDK v3.8.3, including purpose, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron getting-started send-first-message
content: This page explains the Electron sequence from SDK initialization to sending the first message. See each method page for exact signatures. Call Sequence | Step | Required API | Description | | | | | | Initialize | SDK initialization API | Configure apiAddr , wsAddr , data directory, logging, and the connection listener. | | Register listeners | Conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners | Register listeners before login when possible. | | Log in | Login API | Use a token issued by your business backend. Call business APIs only after login succeeds. | | Create a message | createTextMessage / CreateTextMessage and related methods | Create a local message object or JSON payload. | | Send the message | sendMessage / SendMessage | Pass a user ID for one to one chat or a group ID for group chat. | | Log out or uninitialize | Logout and uninitialize APIs | Log out the current user or release global SDK resources. | Initialization Config | Field | Type | Description | | | | | | platformID | number | Platform ID expected by the Electron SDK wrapper. | | apiAddr | string | OpenIM HTTP API address. | | wsAddr | string | OpenIM WebSocket address. | | dataDir | string | Local database, cache, and SDK data directory. | | logLevel | number | SDK log level. | | isLogStandardOutput | boolean | Whether logs are written to standard output. | | logFilePath | string | Log file directory. | Checklist | Check | Expected result | | | | | Initialize once | Avoid recreating the SDK instance repeatedly in the same process. | | Trace operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation. | | Call after login | Most business APIs require initialization and login. | | Validate parameters | Request objects and message objects must match the platform type definitions. | | Keep listeners stable | Listener setters usually replace previous listeners. Add your own fan out layer if needed. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/overview Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/sending messages/overview

title: Overview
description: Explains the SDK lifecycle for Electron SDK v3.8.3, including initialization, login, logout, login status, and runtime state.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron application overview
content: SDK lifecycle covers initialization, login, logout, login status, foreground/background state, and network change notification. Register required listeners after initialization and before login whenever possible. Lifecycle Order | Stage | Method | Description | | | | | | Version record | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/get sdk version | Record the SDK version in startup logs, support tickets, and compatibility checks. | | Initialization | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/init sdk | Create the SDK instance, logging, connection management, local data directory, and base resources. | | Listeners | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/overview | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Login | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a token issued by your backend service. | | State query | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/get login status / Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/get login user id | Decide whether user scoped conversation, message, group, and friend APIs can be called. | | Runtime state | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/set app background status / Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK when app or network state changes. | | Logout | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/logout | End the current user session without destroying the SDK instance. | | Release | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/un init sdk | Release global SDK resources when the app leaves IM capability entirely or tests recreate the instance. | Boundaries | Boundary | Description | | | | | Initialization creates the runtime environment | Successful initialization does not mean the user is logged in. User, friend, group, conversation, and message APIs usually require login. | | Login depends on a backend token | The client should not mint tokens. Refresh expired or invalid tokens through your backend, then log in again. | | Logout is not SDK release | Logout ends the current user session; UnInitSDK releases the global SDK instance. | | operationID should be traceable | Generate operationID values that can correlate client logs, server logs, and support tickets. | Method Index | Method | Description | | | | | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/get sdk version | Get the current core SDK version. | | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/init sdk | Initialize the SDK instance and base resources. | | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/un init sdk | Release the current SDK instance. | | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a backend issued token. | | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/logout | Log out the current user. | | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/set app background status | Notify the SDK that the app entered foreground or background. | | Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK that network status changed. | | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/get login status | Query the current SDK login status. | | Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/get login user id | Get the user ID recorded by the current SDK instance. | Related Pages Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/authenticating a user/authentication Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/error codes

title: Authentication flow
description: Explains the authentication flow, token boundaries, and login-state handling for Electron SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron application authenticating-a-user authentication
content: Authentication uses a user token issued by your backend service. The client initializes the SDK, registers listeners, logs in with userID and token, and keeps UI state in sync with connection events and login status. Authentication Order | Step | Action | Description | | | | | | 1 | Get userID and token | Request the current user's OpenIM userID and token from your backend service. | | 2 | Initialize the SDK | Call Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/init sdk with API address, WebSocket address, platform information, and log settings. | | 3 | Register listeners | Register at least the connection listener. Register conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners before login when those events are needed. | | 4 | Log in | Call Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/login with userID and token. | | 5 | Handle state | Use connection callbacks, Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/get login status , and API results to decide when user scoped APIs are available. | | 6 | Logout or refresh token | When the token expires, becomes invalid, or the user is kicked offline, call Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/methods/logout and refresh the token before logging in again if needed. | Token Boundaries | Scenario | Handling | | | | | Token expired | Refresh the token through your backend, then call login again. | | Token invalid | Check userID, platform, issuing environment, and server time. | | Kicked offline | Notify the user through connection or login state events, then follow your account switch or re login policy. | | User logs out | Call Logout , then clear app level user state and page state. | Related Error Codes | Code | Meaning | | | | | 1501 | Token expired. | | 1502 | Token invalid. | | 1503 | Token malformed. | | 1506 | Token account was kicked offline. | | 1507 | Token does not exist. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/error codes

title: Overview
description: Explains event handler registration timing, event scope, and app-layer dispatch guidance for Electron SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron event-handler overview
content: Event handlers deliver connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV changes to the app layer. Register them before login when possible so initial sync events are not missed. Handler Index | Handler | Use case | | | | | Listen for group events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set group listener | Group info, members, applications, and permission changes. | | Listen for conversation events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set conversation listener | Conversation creation, updates, deletion, unread counts, and drafts. | | Listen for single message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set advanced msg listener | New messages, revocation, read status, and message extension changes. | | Listen for batch message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set batch msg listener | Batched new messages or batched sync results. | | Listen for user events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set user listener | Current user profile and user related state changes. | | Listen for friend events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set friend listener | Friend list, friend info, friend applications, and blacklist changes. | | Listen for custom business events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set custom business listener | Custom business events delivered by the server. | | Listen for message KV changes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/methods/set message kv info listener | Message extension key value additions, updates, and deletion. | Registration Guidance | Guidance | Description | | | | | Register before login | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Fan out in app code | If multiple pages consume the same event type, dispatch it through an app level event bus or state store. | | Avoid accidental replacement | Most Set Listener calls replace the listener stored for that event category. Repeated registration can overwrite the previous listener. | | Keep cache in sync | Listener callbacks should update local cache and UI state, then notify screens that need to refresh. |

title: Overview
description: User, friends, blacklist, and presence method index for Electron SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron user overview
content: This page covers user profile, user command, presence subscription, friend, and blacklist APIs. Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. User Profile And Presence | Method | Description | | | | | Get self user info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/get self user info | Get the current user's profile for profile pages, startup refresh, or account switching. | | Update self info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/set self info | Update the current user's profile, such as nickname, avatar, or extension fields. | | Get users info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/get users info | Batch query public user profiles for conversation lists, message sender info, or friend refresh. | | Add user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/add user command | Add a lightweight user side command or marker. | | Delete user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/delete user command | Delete a user command by type and UUID. | | Get all user commands /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/get all user commands | Get all user commands under a specified type. | | Subscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/subscribe users status | Subscribe to online status for a group of users. | | Unsubscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/unsubscribe users status | Stop receiving online status for specified users. | | Get subscribed user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/get subscribe users status | Get the current status snapshot for subscribed users. | | Get user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/methods/get user status | Query online status for specified users. | Friends | Method | Description | | | | | Get specified friends info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/get specified friends info | Get profile details for specified friends. | | Get friend list /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/get friend list | Get the current user's full friend list. | | Get friend list by page /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/get friend list page | Page through the current user's friend list. | | Search friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/search friends | Search friends by keyword. | | Check friendship /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/check friend | Check friend relationship status. | | Add friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/add friend | Send a friend request. | | Update friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/update friends | Update friend profile fields controlled by the current user. | | Delete friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/delete friend | Delete a friend relationship. | | Get received friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as recipient | Get friend requests received by the current user. | | Get sent friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as applicant | Get friend requests sent by the current user. | | Accept friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/accept friend application | Accept a received friend request. | | Refuse friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/refuse friend application | Refuse a received friend request. | | Get unhandled friend application count /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/friend/methods/get friend application unhandled count | Get the number of unhandled friend requests. | Blacklist | Method | Description | | | | | Add to blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/blacklist/methods/add black | Add a user to the blacklist. | | Get blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/blacklist/methods/get black list | Get the current blacklist. | | Remove from blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/user/blacklist/methods/remove black | Remove a user from the blacklist. |

title: Overview
description: Group method index for Electron SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron group overview
content: This page covers OpenIM group creation, group profile management, group applications, membership, members, moderation, and ownership transfer. Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. Managing Groups | Method | Description | | | | | Create group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/group/managing groups/create group | Create a group and optionally set initial members and administrators. | | Update group info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/group/managing groups/set group info | Update group profile fields such as name, announcement, introduction, and verification mode. | | Get group info title: Overview
description: Conversation and message method index for Electron SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron message overview
content: This page covers conversations and messages: conversation lists, conversation settings, typing state, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and local messages. Main Flow | Stage | Methods | | | | | Create message | Use Create Message methods to create local message objects. | | Send message | Send with Send message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/sending messages/send message or Send message without OSS /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/sending messages/send message not oss . | | Receive message | Register message listeners from Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/event handler/overview . | | Query history | Use history and local search APIs under retrieving messages. | | Maintain read state | Mark conversation or message IDs as read. | Conversation Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Listing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/listing conversations/get all conversation list | Get all, paged, single, multiple, search, and generate conversation ID. | | Managing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/managing conversations/set conversation | Set conversation options, hide conversations, and update drafts. | | Conversation state /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/conversation state/get total unread msg count | Unread counts, input state, and typing status. | Message Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Creating messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/creating messages/create text message | Text, @ text, location, custom, quote, card, face, forward, merge, media, and file messages. | | Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/sending messages/send message | Send regular messages or send without OSS. | | Retrieving messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/retrieving messages/get advanced history message list | History message lists, reverse history, message list lookup, and local search. | | Managing messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/managing messages/revoke message | Revoke messages and set local message extension. | | Read status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/read status/mark conversation message as read | Mark conversation messages, all conversations, or selected message IDs as read. | | Deleting messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/deleting messages/delete message | Delete local messages, delete server messages, clear messages, and delete conversation messages. | | Local messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/message/local messages/insert single message to local storage | Insert local single chat or group messages. |

Keep operationID values traceable and validate platform specific parameters before calling the SDK. Related Pages Electron SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/electron/overview

title: OpenIM SDK for Mini Program
description: OpenIM Mini Program SDK entry point for supported mini program runtimes.
keywords: sdk miniprogram v4 chat sdk v4 miniprogram overview
content: Overview Mini Program integrations follow the WASM compatible core API model for user login, conversations, messages, groups, and events, while the runtime adapter must respect each mini program platform's storage, networking, upload, and websocket restrictions. Compatibility with WASM Use the WASM SDK documentation for shared OpenIM concepts and method level behavior. Mini Program pages should document only the runtime differences: initialization entry, asset handling, request domain configuration, file upload limits, and background lifecycle. Runtime notes Configure allowed request and websocket domains before testing login. Verify local storage quota and cleanup behavior for conversation and message cache. Test file messages with the target platform's upload API and file size limits. Keep token issuance on a trusted backend and pass only short lived credentials to the client. Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview User authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message

title: OpenIM React Native SDK overview
description: React Native SDK v3.8.3 documentation organized by OpenIM SDK capabilities.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native overview
content: This documentation set follows the current OpenIM SDK capability model and the public methods exposed by React Native SDK v3.8.3. Each method page covers the platform call shape, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries. The pages describe how the platform wrapper maps to OpenIM core capabilities. Platform method names and parameter wrappers can differ; when the wrapper differs from the core export, follow the platform SDK v3.8.3 signature shown on the method page. Integration Model | Model | Description | | | | | Initialization | Configure the API address, WebSocket address, local data directory, logging, and connection listener. | | Login state | Register listeners before login when possible, then call the login API with a backend issued token. | | Async result | Platform methods return results through Promise, Future, or callback patterns depending on the SDK. | | Message creation | Create local message objects with Create Message methods before sending them. | | operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation so client and server logs can be correlated. | Capability Pages | Page | Scope | | | | | Integration flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/getting started/send first message | Initialization, listeners, login, and the first message sequence. | | SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/overview | SDK initialization, login, logout, login status, and foreground/background state. | | Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/authenticating a user/authentication | Token handling, connection listener behavior, login status, and logout boundaries. | | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/overview | Connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV callbacks. | | User /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/overview | User profiles, user commands, and presence subscription. | | Friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/overview friends | Friend list, friend info, friend search, friend applications, and relationship updates. | | Blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/overview blacklist | Add, list, and remove blacklist entries. | | Group /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/group/overview | Group creation, applications, membership, members, moderation, and ownership. | | Conversations and messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/overview | Conversation lists, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and typing state. | | Push notifications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/push notifications/overview | FCM token and application badge updates. | | Logger /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/logger/overview | SDK log configuration, writing client logs, and uploading logs. | | File upload /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/file/overview | Upload files through the object storage path configured for the SDK. | | Models /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/models/overview | Common models, enums, listeners, and callbacks. | | Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/error codes | SDK core, server, and WebSocket protocol marker error code enumeration. | | Unsupported or unexposed capabilities /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/deprecated | Capability boundaries that are not exposed by the current SDK. | Currently Unavailable Capabilities React Native SDK v3.8.3 does not expose standalone methods for public group variants, polls, scheduled messages, message threads, translation, independent reports, group metadata, or metacounters. Add these pages only after the platform SDK exposes corresponding methods.

title: React Native integration flow
description: Explains the React Native integration flow method in React Native SDK v3.8.3, including purpose, parameters, return value, and usage boundaries.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native getting-started send-first-message
content: This page explains the React Native sequence from SDK initialization to sending the first message. See each method page for exact signatures. Call Sequence | Step | Required API | Description | | | | | | Initialize | SDK initialization API | Configure apiAddr , wsAddr , data directory, logging, and the connection listener. | | Register listeners | Conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners | Register listeners before login when possible. | | Log in | Login API | Use a token issued by your business backend. Call business APIs only after login succeeds. | | Create a message | createTextMessage / CreateTextMessage and related methods | Create a local message object or JSON payload. | | Send the message | sendMessage / SendMessage | Pass a user ID for one to one chat or a group ID for group chat. | | Log out or uninitialize | Logout and uninitialize APIs | Log out the current user or release global SDK resources. | Initialization Config | Field | Type | Description | | | | | | platformID | number | Platform ID expected by the React Native SDK wrapper. | | apiAddr | string | OpenIM HTTP API address. | | wsAddr | string | OpenIM WebSocket address. | | dataDir | string | Local database, cache, and SDK data directory. | | logLevel | number | SDK log level. | | isLogStandardOutput | boolean | Whether logs are written to standard output. | | logFilePath | string | Log file directory. | Checklist | Check | Expected result | | | | | Initialize once | Avoid recreating the SDK instance repeatedly in the same process. | | Trace operationID | Generate a traceable operationID for each business operation. | | Call after login | Most business APIs require initialization and login. | | Validate parameters | Request objects and message objects must match the platform type definitions. | | Keep listeners stable | Listener setters usually replace previous listeners. Add your own fan out layer if needed. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/overview Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/sending messages/overview

title: Overview
description: Explains the SDK lifecycle for React Native SDK v3.8.3, including initialization, login, logout, login status, and runtime state.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native application overview
content: SDK lifecycle covers initialization, login, logout, login status, foreground/background state, and network change notification. Register required listeners after initialization and before login whenever possible. Lifecycle Order | Stage | Method | Description | | | | | | Version record | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/get sdk version | Record the SDK version in startup logs, support tickets, and compatibility checks. | | Initialization | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/init sdk | Create the SDK instance, logging, connection management, local data directory, and base resources. | | Listeners | Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/overview | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Login | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a token issued by your backend service. | | State query | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/get login status / Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/get login user id | Decide whether user scoped conversation, message, group, and friend APIs can be called. | | Runtime state | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/set app background status / Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK when app or network state changes. | | Logout | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/logout | End the current user session without destroying the SDK instance. | | Release | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/un init sdk | Release global SDK resources when the app leaves IM capability entirely or tests recreate the instance. | Boundaries | Boundary | Description | | | | | Initialization creates the runtime environment | Successful initialization does not mean the user is logged in. User, friend, group, conversation, and message APIs usually require login. | | Login depends on a backend token | The client should not mint tokens. Refresh expired or invalid tokens through your backend, then log in again. | | Logout is not SDK release | Logout ends the current user session; UnInitSDK releases the global SDK instance. | | operationID should be traceable | Generate operationID values that can correlate client logs, server logs, and support tickets. | Method Index | Method | Description | | | | | Get SDK version /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/get sdk version | Get the current core SDK version. | | Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/init sdk | Initialize the SDK instance and base resources. | | Uninitialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/un init sdk | Release the current SDK instance. | | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/login | Log in a user with a backend issued token. | | Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/logout | Log out the current user. | | Set foreground/background status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/set app background status | Notify the SDK that the app entered foreground or background. | | Network status changed /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/network status changed | Notify the SDK that network status changed. | | Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/get login status | Query the current SDK login status. | | Get login user ID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/get login user id | Get the user ID recorded by the current SDK instance. | Related Pages Authentication flow /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/authenticating a user/authentication Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/error codes

title: Authentication flow
description: Explains the authentication flow, token boundaries, and login-state handling for React Native SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native application authenticating-a-user authentication
content: Authentication uses a user token issued by your backend service. The client initializes the SDK, registers listeners, logs in with userID and token, and keeps UI state in sync with connection events and login status. Authentication Order | Step | Action | Description | | | | | | 1 | Get userID and token | Request the current user's OpenIM userID and token from your backend service. | | 2 | Initialize the SDK | Call Initialize the SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/init sdk with API address, WebSocket address, platform information, and log settings. | | 3 | Register listeners | Register at least the connection listener. Register conversation, message, friend, group, and user listeners before login when those events are needed. | | 4 | Log in | Call Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/login with userID and token. | | 5 | Handle state | Use connection callbacks, Get login status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/get login status , and API results to decide when user scoped APIs are available. | | 6 | Logout or refresh token | When the token expires, becomes invalid, or the user is kicked offline, call Log out /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/methods/logout and refresh the token before logging in again if needed. | Token Boundaries | Scenario | Handling | | | | | Token expired | Refresh the token through your backend, then call login again. | | Token invalid | Check userID, platform, issuing environment, and server time. | | Kicked offline | Notify the user through connection or login state events, then follow your account switch or re login policy. | | User logs out | Call Logout , then clear app level user state and page state. | Related Error Codes | Code | Meaning | | | | | 1501 | Token expired. | | 1502 | Token invalid. | | 1503 | Token malformed. | | 1506 | Token account was kicked offline. | | 1507 | Token does not exist. | Related Pages SDK lifecycle /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/application/overview Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/overview Error codes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/error codes

title: Overview
description: Explains event handler registration timing, event scope, and app-layer dispatch guidance for React Native SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native event-handler overview
content: Event handlers deliver connection, user, friend, group, conversation, message, custom business, and message KV changes to the app layer. Register them before login when possible so initial sync events are not missed. Handler Index | Handler | Use case | | | | | Listen for group events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set group listener | Group info, members, applications, and permission changes. | | Listen for conversation events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set conversation listener | Conversation creation, updates, deletion, unread counts, and drafts. | | Listen for single message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set advanced msg listener | New messages, revocation, read status, and message extension changes. | | Listen for batch message events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set batch msg listener | Batched new messages or batched sync results. | | Listen for user events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set user listener | Current user profile and user related state changes. | | Listen for friend events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set friend listener | Friend list, friend info, friend applications, and blacklist changes. | | Listen for custom business events /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set custom business listener | Custom business events delivered by the server. | | Listen for message KV changes /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/methods/set message kv info listener | Message extension key value additions, updates, and deletion. | Registration Guidance | Guidance | Description | | | | | Register before login | Register connection, message, conversation, friend, group, and user listeners before login when possible. | | Fan out in app code | If multiple pages consume the same event type, dispatch it through an app level event bus or state store. | | Avoid accidental replacement | Most Set Listener calls replace the listener stored for that event category. Repeated registration can overwrite the previous listener. | | Keep cache in sync | Listener callbacks should update local cache and UI state, then notify screens that need to refresh. |

title: Overview
description: User, friends, blacklist, and presence method index for React Native SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native user overview
content: This page covers user profile, user command, presence subscription, friend, and blacklist APIs. Each method has its own page for parameters, returns, and usage boundaries. User Profile And Presence | Method | Description | | | | | Get self user info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/get self user info | Get the current user's profile for profile pages, startup refresh, or account switching. | | Update self info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/set self info | Update the current user's profile, such as nickname, avatar, or extension fields. | | Get users info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/get users info | Batch query public user profiles for conversation lists, message sender info, or friend refresh. | | Add user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/add user command | Add a lightweight user side command or marker. | | Delete user command /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/delete user command | Delete a user command by type and UUID. | | Get all user commands /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/get all user commands | Get all user commands under a specified type. | | Subscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/subscribe users status | Subscribe to online status for a group of users. | | Unsubscribe users status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/unsubscribe users status | Stop receiving online status for specified users. | | Get subscribed user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/get subscribe users status | Get the current status snapshot for subscribed users. | | Get user status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/methods/get user status | Query online status for specified users. | Friends | Method | Description | | | | | Get specified friends info /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/get specified friends info | Get profile details for specified friends. | | Get friend list /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/get friend list | Get the current user's full friend list. | | Get friend list by page /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/get friend list page | Page through the current user's friend list. | | Search friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/search friends | Search friends by keyword. | | Check friendship /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/check friend | Check friend relationship status. | | Add friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/add friend | Send a friend request. | | Update friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/update friends | Update friend profile fields controlled by the current user. | | Delete friend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/delete friend | Delete a friend relationship. | | Get received friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as recipient | Get friend requests received by the current user. | | Get sent friend applications /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as applicant | Get friend requests sent by the current user. | | Accept friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/accept friend application | Accept a received friend request. | | Refuse friend application /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/refuse friend application | Refuse a received friend request. | | Get unhandled friend application count /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/friend/methods/get friend application unhandled count | Get the number of unhandled friend requests. | Blacklist | Method | Description | | | | | Add to blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/blacklist/methods/add black | Add a user to the blacklist. | | Get blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/blacklist/methods/get black list | Get the current blacklist. | | Remove from blacklist /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/user/blacklist/methods/remove black | Remove a user from the blacklist. |

title: Overview
description: Conversation and message method index for React Native SDK v3.8.3.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native message overview
content: This page covers conversations and messages: conversation lists, conversation settings, typing state, message creation, sending, history, read status, deletion, search, and local messages. Main Flow | Stage | Methods | | | | | Create message | Use Create Message methods to create local message objects. | | Send message | Send with Send message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/sending messages/send message or Send message without OSS /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/sending messages/send message not oss . | | Receive message | Register message listeners from Event handlers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/event handler/overview . | | Query history | Use history and local search APIs under retrieving messages. | | Maintain read state | Mark conversation or message IDs as read. | Conversation Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Listing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/listing conversations/get all conversation list | Get all, paged, single, multiple, search, and generate conversation ID. | | Managing conversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/managing conversations/set conversation | Set conversation options, hide conversations, and update drafts. | | Conversation state /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/conversation state/get total unread msg count | Unread counts, input state, and typing status. | Message Methods | Group | Scope | | | | | Creating messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/creating messages/create text message | Text, @ text, location, custom, quote, card, face, forward, merge, media, and file messages. | | Sending messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/sending messages/send message | Send regular messages or send without OSS. | | Retrieving messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/retrieving messages/get advanced history message list | History message lists, reverse history, message list lookup, and local search. | | Managing messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/managing messages/revoke message | Revoke messages and set local message extension. | | Read status /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/read status/mark conversation message as read | Mark conversation messages, all conversations, or selected message IDs as read. | | Deleting messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/deleting messages/delete message | Delete local messages, delete server messages, clear messages, and delete conversation messages. | | Local messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/message/local messages/insert single message to local storage | Insert local single chat or group messages. |

content: OnPutFileListener is a common type used by React Native SDK v3.8.3. Use this page to understand where the type appears and how it connects to method parameters, return values, and event callbacks. Usage | Scenario | Description | | | | | Method parameters | Some SDK methods accept this type or a request object with the same shape. | | Return values | Query methods and callbacks may return this type or a list of this type. | | Event handling | Listener callbacks may include this type when related data changes. | Usage Notes Treat ID fields such as userID , groupID , conversationID , and clientMsgID as stable identifiers. Optional extension fields are usually JSON strings or platform specific maps. Validate them before using them in UI logic. Prefer SDK update methods instead of mutating local objects only. title: Overview
description: Overview for the OpenIM Platform API.
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 logs overview
content: 日志模块面向客户端本地日志的统一管理场景设计。业务方可以在 App、Web、桌面端等客户端采集本地日志，先将日志文件上传到可访问的对象地址，再通过日志接口登记文件 URL、平台、版本和扩展信息，便于在管理后台、客服排障或问题反馈流程中统一检索和处理。 这个模块不用于暴露 OpenIM 服务端运行日志，也不负责直接接收日志文件内容；它管理的是客户端日志文件的索引和元数据。 能力范围 | 能力 | 说明 | | | | | 上传日志 | 登记业务方已采集并上传的客户端本地日志 URL 和客户端环境信息。 | | 搜索日志 | 按关键字、时间范围和分页参数检索客户端日志记录。 | | 删除日志 | 按日志 ID 删除客户端日志索引记录。 | 常用接口 上传日志记录 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/logs/upload logs 搜索日志记录 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/logs/search logs 删除日志记录 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/logs/delete logs 接入建议 日志接口适合管理后台、客服排障和客户端问题反馈流程使用。调用上传接口前，应先由客户端或业务服务完成日志文件上传，并确保传入的 URL 可由 OpenIM 服务端访问。 客户端本地日志可能包含用户环境、设备信息和业务上下文，建议业务方在自己的产品流程中控制日志采集入口、用户授权、保留周期和访问权限。 相关页面 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 认证 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/auth/overview 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes

title: Upload logs
description: Upload logs with the OpenIM Platform API, including endpoint, request body, response fields, and error handling.
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 logs upload-logs
content: 使用 上传日志记录 从可信后端调用 OpenIM 日志接口。业务方可以先在客户端采集本地日志并上传到对象存储或自己的文件服务，再通过此接口登记日志文件 URL、平台、版本和扩展信息，便于后续在后台统一检索。请先在 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 中配置 API 地址和 Token；接口参数通过请求头和 JSON 请求体传递。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /third/logs/upload 请求示例 curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /third/logs/upload" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: $ OPERATION ID " \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "platform": 2, "fileURLs": "filename": "openim.log", "URL": "https://example.com/logs/openim.log" , "appFramework": "flutter", "version": "1.0.0", "ex": "" ' 安全提示：管理端 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。 参数 此接口通过请求头传入链路追踪信息和鉴权凭证，通过 JSON 请求体传递业务参数。 请求头 | Header | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | | operationID | 是 | string | 每次请求的唯一链路追踪 ID，建议由后端生成并写入日志。 | | token | 是 | string | APP 管理员 Token 或具备对应权限的用户 Token；仅保存在可信后端服务中。 | | Content Type: application/json | 是 | string | 请求体为 JSON 时必须设置。 | 请求体 "platform": 2, "fileURLs": "filename": "openim.log", "URL": "https://example.com/logs/openim.log" , "appFramework": "flutter", "version": "1.0.0", "ex": "" | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | | platform | 是 | int | 日志来源终端平台 ID。 | | fileURLs | 是 | array | 已上传日志文件列表。 | | fileURLs .filename | 是 | string | 日志文件名。 | | fileURLs .URL | 是 | string | 日志文件可访问 URL。 | | appFramework | 否 | string | 客户端框架，例如 flutter、react native。 | | version | 否 | string | 客户端或应用版本号。 | | ex | 否 | string | 扩展字段。 | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "" 响应参数 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | errCode | int | 业务错误码，0 表示成功。 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息，成功时通常为空。 | | errDlt | string | 错误详细信息，成功时通常为空。 | 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或调用账号没有该接口权限。 | 重新获取有效 Token，并在可信后端调用接口。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表和限制说明检查字段。 | 权限和限制 写入日志索引记录；日志文件本身应先由调用方上传到可访问地址。 platform 和 fileURLs 必填。 fileURLs .URL 必须是服务端可访问的日志文件地址。 所有数组型请求参数建议控制在 1000 个元素以内。 相关页面 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 认证接口 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/auth/tokens/get admin token 日志概述 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/logs/overview 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes

title: Search logs
description: Search logs with the OpenIM Platform API, including endpoint, request body, response fields, and error handling.
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 logs search-logs
content: 使用 搜索日志记录 从可信后端调用 OpenIM 日志接口。按关键字、时间范围和分页参数检索业务方已登记的客户端本地日志记录，用于后台排障、客服定位和问题反馈跟进。请先在 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 中配置 API 地址和 Token；接口参数通过请求头和 JSON 请求体传递。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /third/logs/search 请求示例 curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /third/logs/search" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: $ OPERATION ID " \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "keyword": "user 001", "startTime": 1719800000000, "endTime": 1719886400000, "pagination": "pageNumber": 1, "showNumber": 20 ' 安全提示：管理端 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。 参数 此接口通过请求头传入链路追踪信息和鉴权凭证，通过 JSON 请求体传递业务参数。 请求头 | Header | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | | operationID | 是 | string | 每次请求的唯一链路追踪 ID，建议由后端生成并写入日志。 | | token | 是 | string | APP 管理员 Token 或具备对应权限的用户 Token；仅保存在可信后端服务中。 | | Content Type: application/json | 是 | string | 请求体为 JSON 时必须设置。 | 请求体 "keyword": "user 001", "startTime": 1719800000000, "endTime": 1719886400000, "pagination": "pageNumber": 1, "showNumber": 20 | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | | keyword | 否 | string | 搜索关键字，可匹配日志相关字段。 | | startTime | 否 | int | 起始时间，Unix 毫秒时间戳；与 endTime 同时为 0 时服务端使用默认范围。 | | endTime | 否 | int | 结束时间，Unix 毫秒时间戳。 | | pagination | 是 | object | 分页参数。 | | pagination.pageNumber | 是 | int | 页码，从 1 开始。 | | pagination.showNumber | 是 | int | 每页数量，必须大于 0。 | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "", "data": "logsInfos": "userID": "user 001", "platform": "Android", "url": "https://example.com/logs/openim.log", "createTime": 1719800000000, "nickname": "Tom", "logID": "1234567890", "filename": "openim.log", "systemType": "", "ex": "", "version": "1.0.0" , "total": 1 响应参数 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | errCode | int | 业务错误码，0 表示成功。 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息，成功时通常为空。 | | errDlt | string | 错误详细信息，成功时通常为空。 | | data | object | 接口返回数据。 | | data.logsInfos | array | 日志记录列表。 | | data.logsInfos .userID | string | 上传日志的用户 ID。 | | data.logsInfos .platform | string | 日志来源平台名称。 | | data.logsInfos .url | string | 日志文件 URL。 | | data.logsInfos .createTime | int | 日志记录创建时间，Unix 毫秒时间戳。 | | data.logsInfos .nickname | string | 上传用户昵称。 | | data.logsInfos .logID | string | 日志 ID。 | | data.logsInfos .filename | string | 日志文件名。 | | data.logsInfos .systemType | string | 系统类型。 | | data.logsInfos .ex | string | 扩展字段。 | | data.logsInfos .version | string | 客户端或应用版本号。 | | data.total | int | 符合条件的日志总数。 | 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或调用账号没有该接口权限。 | 重新获取有效 Token，并在可信后端调用接口。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表和限制说明检查字段。 | 权限和限制 只读查询，不删除或修改日志记录。 必须使用 APP 管理员 Token 调用。 startTime 不能大于 endTime 。 pagination 必填。 所有数组型请求参数建议控制在 1000 个元素以内。 相关页面 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 认证接口 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/auth/tokens/get admin token 日志概述 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/logs/overview 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes

title: Delete logs
description: Delete logs with the OpenIM Platform API, including endpoint, request body, response fields, and error handling.
keywords: platform-api v3 chat platform-api v3 logs delete-logs
content: 使用 删除日志记录 从可信后端调用 OpenIM 日志接口。按日志 ID 删除后台保存的客户端本地日志索引记录，适合在日志过期、问题处理完成或业务方主动清理时调用。请先在 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 中配置 API 地址和 Token；接口参数通过请求头和 JSON 请求体传递。 HTTP 请求 POST API ADDRESS /third/logs/delete 请求示例 curl request POST "$ API ADDRESS /third/logs/delete" \ header "Content Type: application/json; charset=utf 8" \ header "operationID: $ OPERATION ID " \ header "token: $ ADMIN TOKEN " \ data raw ' "logIDs": "1234567890" ' 安全提示：管理端 Token 只能保存在可信后端服务中，不能下发到客户端或写入前端代码。 参数 此接口通过请求头传入链路追踪信息和鉴权凭证，通过 JSON 请求体传递业务参数。 请求头 | Header | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | | operationID | 是 | string | 每次请求的唯一链路追踪 ID，建议由后端生成并写入日志。 | | token | 是 | string | APP 管理员 Token 或具备对应权限的用户 Token；仅保存在可信后端服务中。 | | Content Type: application/json | 是 | string | 请求体为 JSON 时必须设置。 | 请求体 "logIDs": "1234567890" | 参数名 | 是否必填 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | | logIDs | 是 | array | 需要删除的日志 ID 列表。 | 响应 请求被 OpenIM 正常处理时通常返回 200 OK 。业务是否成功以响应体中的 errCode 为准； errCode === 0 表示成功，非 0 表示业务错误。 "errCode": 0, "errMsg": "", "errDlt": "" 响应参数 | 参数名 | 类型 | 说明 | | | | | | errCode | int | 业务错误码，0 表示成功。 | | errMsg | string | 错误简要信息，成功时通常为空。 | | errDlt | string | 错误详细信息，成功时通常为空。 | 错误 如果请求失败，OpenIM 返回错误对象。更多错误码说明见 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes 。 "errCode": 1004, "errMsg": "RecordNotFoundError", "errDlt": ": 1004 RecordNotFoundError" | 错误场景 | 可能原因 | 处理方式 | | | | | | 鉴权失败 | token 缺失、过期，或调用账号没有该接口权限。 | 重新获取有效 Token，并在可信后端调用接口。 | | 链路追踪困难 | operationID 缺失或在大量请求中重复使用。 | 为每次请求生成独立 operationID ，并在服务端日志中保留。 | | 参数校验失败 | 请求体字段类型、必填字段或枚举值不符合接口要求。 | 对照请求体参数表和限制说明检查字段。 | 权限和限制 删除指定日志索引记录。 必须使用 APP 管理员 Token 调用。 logIDs 必填且不能为空。 所有数组型请求参数建议控制在 1000 个元素以内。 相关页面 接入准备 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/prepare to use api 认证接口 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/auth/tokens/get admin token 日志概述 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/logs/overview 错误码 /docs/chat/platform api/v3/error codes
